_doc.purpose: Single config for the autonomous promotion engine. All three channel skills (snipe/reddit-snipe/promote) and the orchestrator read knobs from here. Edit caps/posture here, never inside a SKILL.md.

posture.shadow: Draft + critique + record only. NEVER posts. Use for the first runs to inspect what the engine WOULD post.
posture.batch: Engine drafts/critiques/guards, pushes ONE Feishu card listing what it is about to post, waits veto_window_hours; if no ABORT reply, it posts the batch. Near-zero touch + a circuit breaker. DEFAULT.
posture.auto: Posts immediately after critic-pass + guard-pass. Feishu card is a notification only. Fully unattended.

_doc.kill_switch: Set true to halt ALL posting on the next run regardless of posture. The fastest safe stop.
_doc.first_run_advice: Leave posture=shadow for 2-3 cycles, read the Feishu reports, then move to batch, then auto once you trust the drafts.

github._deny_repos_why: Upstream agent body, not a same-space peer bridge: highest scrutiny (staff triage) + commenting our tool there is off-strategy. The registry had 14 comments here historically; stop adding.

twitter._link_in_self_reply_why: X downranks reach of posts containing external links. Main reply = pitch + `npx pikiloom@latest` (native, no link); GitHub link posted as a self-reply underneath.

content_variation._min_distinct_ratio_why: Reject a draft whose bag-of-words overlap with a prior POSTED draft on the same channel exceeds (1 - ratio) = 0.45 Jaccard. Calibrated: reworded boilerplate (incl. same-angle paraphrases) scores 0.53-0.65 -> caught; genuinely distinct drafts score <0.10 -> pass. Stops boilerplate-across-targets, the top spam-classifier trigger on X and GitHub.

critic._max_revisions_why: draft -> critic -> at most one revision -> {pass | drop}. Replaces the human review gate with an autonomous adversarial pass.

backoff._why: If a recent post on a channel was hidden/removed/locked, pause that channel for the cooldown. A hidden comment is a warning shot; arguing it escalates to a report.

measure._traffic_note: GitHub Traffic API keeps only 14 days; measure.py must run daily to persist history.
